title: Scope A T3: implement rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded + 50MB soft-warn + events_rotated audit event
description: Sliding-window-by-age rotation primitive: read main ledger → partition by ts vs cutoff (now - retention_days * 86400_000) → append archived lines to events.archive/events-rotated-{YYYY-MM-DD}.jsonl (same-day appends to same file) → atomicWrite kept tail → emit events_rotated audit event as first line of new main. Also adds 50MB soft stderr warn on append paths and reads retention from fabric_event_retention_days config (default 30, accepts 7/30/90).

files.change: Add new event shape `events_rotated` with fields `cutoff_ts: string (ISO)`, `archived_count: number`, `kept_count: number`, `archive_path: string (relative .fabric/events.archive/...)`. Add to discriminated union at line 432. Include `source` field consistent with peer events.
Add parse-fixture test for `events_rotated` including required + optional fields; assert discriminated union rejects unknown fields per existing pattern.

Add `rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded(projectRoot: string, opts?: { now?: Date; retentionDays?: number }): Promise<{ rotated: boolean; archivedCount: number; keptCount: number; archivePath?: string }>`. Wraps the full read-partition-write in `runExclusive(getEventLedgerPath(projectRoot), async () => ...)`. Reads retention from `readFabricConfig().fabric_event_retention_days ?? 30` when opts.retentionDays omitted. Computes cutoff_ts = (opts.now ?? new Date()).getTime() - retention_days * 86400_000. Reads ledger (returning early with rotated:false when no lines older than cutoff). Partitions into [archived, kept] by ts field. Ensures .fabric/events.archive/ exists. Builds archive filename `events-rotated-${YYYY-MM-DD}.jsonl` using rotation-date (opts.now). Appends archived NDJSON lines via fs.appendFile (creates if absent). Builds new main content = `events_rotated` audit JSONL + '\n' + kept lines. atomicWriteText to main path. Returns counts.

After successful append, run `statSync(path).size` and if > 50 * 1024 * 1024, emit one-shot `process.stderr.write('fabric: events.jsonl > 50MB, run "fab doctor --fix" to rotate\n')`. Gate the one-shot via a process-scoped flag so warnings don't spam (e.g., module-level `let warnedOversize = false`).

Add optional field `fabric_event_retention_days?: number` (z.union of literals 7 / 30 / 90 OR z.number().int().positive() — pick stricter literal union per cite-policy precedent). Default 30 applied at consumer site.

Tests for rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded: (1) no-op when all lines within retention window; (2) partitions correctly when some lines older than cutoff; (3) same-day re-run appends to same archive file (not overwrite); (4) new-day rotation creates new archive file with new YYYY-MM-DD; (5) first line of new main is events_rotated audit with correct counts; (6) emits events_rotated through schema validator (round-trip); (7) reads retention from config (mock readFabricConfig returning 7) and applies it; (8) 50MB soft-warn one-shot fires exactly once.

implementation: Add `events_rotated` schema first (smallest unit, drives type-safety for impl below)
Add 50MB soft-warn one-shot in append path with module-level guard
Write 8 unit tests covering all scenarios
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/server test event-ledger` and `pnpm --filter @fabric/shared test event-ledger` to confirm green

reference.pattern: Existing event-ledger schema discriminated union + appendEventLedgerEvent flow
reference.examples: `event_ledger_truncated` event shape (line 107) is the closest precedent — copy structure for `events_rotated`

criteria: `events_rotated` enum + discriminated union member parses round-trip via existing schema test pattern
`rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded` is exported and wraps work inside `runExclusive(ledgerPath, ...)`
Same-day re-rotation appends to the same `events-rotated-{date}.jsonl` archive (verified by file size growth in test)
Post-rotation main file's first line parses as `events_rotated` with archived_count + kept_count matching test setup
Retention reads from `fabric_event_retention_days` config key with default 30 (test mocks 7 and verifies behavior)
50MB soft-warn writes to stderr exactly once per process when threshold crossed

rationale.chosen_approach: Sliding window by age + same-day archive append + audit event as new main first-line
decision_factors: Archive filename groups same-day rotations naturally
Audit event provides forensic continuity post-rotation
rationale.tradeoffs: Same-day archive can grow unboundedly if rotation runs many times in one day (acceptable; cap by reducing retention if user hits this)

manual_checks: Manually inspect a post-rotation events.jsonl: first line is JSON with event_type=events_rotated
Run with retention_days=0 (force full rotation) on a test fixture and confirm main becomes audit-only
